title: Beyond modernisation: The new playbook for enterprise reinvention
author: ET Online
short_desc: Maran Nagarajan, CEO of KaarTech, traces the company’s evolution from a specialized enterprise services firm into a global AI-led transformation player.
long_desc: As companies worldwide endeavor to transform in an AI-first economy, the dialogue is evolving from digital transformation as a buzzword to transformation as an actual, measurable business outcome. Speaking to ET Digital, KaarTech's CEO, Maran Nagarajan, reflected on the strategic gambles, execution rigor, and AI-based operating frameworks that fueled the company's ascent. He also outlined what it takes for organizations to progress from incremental modernisation to significant reinvention. Edited excerpts.The Economic Times (ET): KaarTech began as a four-founder venture in 2005. What pivotal decisions or inflection points transformed from a services firm into a global AI-led transformation company?Maran Nagarajan (MN): One early decision that shaped our trajectory was focusing deeply on enterprise platforms rather than operating as a generic IT services firm. That allowed us to build strong domain expertise and long-term client relationships across industries. Over time, we saw that…

title: From control tower to brain of space: India’s trillion-dollar orbit
author: ET CONTRIBUTORS
short_desc: India today accounts for less than 3% of the global space economy, despite its proven technical capability. Closing that gap will require moving beyond launch excellence into high-value data orchestration and intelligent services.
long_desc: When Neil Armstrong landed on the moon in 1969, he declared his small step into space as a “giant leap for mankind”. Over five decades later, the exponential growth of the space economy is opening a whole new world of economic opportunities for humanity.The global space economy, currently estimated at over $600 billion, is projected to cross the $1-trillion mark as early as 2032. But what is more important than the number is where the value lies. Nearly three-fourths of this opportunity will sit downstream in data, analytics, applications, and AI-enabled services, not in rockets.India today accounts for less than 3% of the global space economy, despite its proven technical capability. Closing that gap will require moving beyond launch excellence into high-value data orchestration and intelligent services. The space race is no longer a ‘theatre of prestige’ defined by who plants the next flag on the moon, billionaire astronauts, or distant Mars missions. In the era of artificial…

title: Engineering exports top $10 billion in Feb 2026 despite global headwinds, shipments to China double
author: ET Online
short_desc: Indian engineering exports achieved double-digit growth in February 2026, surpassing ten billion US dollars. This positive trend occurred despite global geopolitical conflicts and economic slowdowns. Key markets like China, Saudi Arabia, and the UK showed strong performance.
long_desc: New Delhi: Despite geopolitical conflicts and economic slowdown in several regions of the world, India's engineering goods exports recorded double-digit growth in February 2026 and stayed above US$10 billion.While engineering shipments to the US and UAE declined in February, some of the key countries that saw positive growth during this period included China, Saudi Arabia, the UK, Singapore, and the Republic of Korea.Out of 25 key markets for Indian engineering goods exports, positive growth was seen in as many as 17 countries.Engineering goods exports to the US, India's top market, fell 4.9% year-on-year to US$1.57 billion in February 2026 as compared to US$1.65 billion in the corresponding period last year. Shipments to the second largest market of the UAE declined 14% year-on-year to…

title: AION-Tech Solutions elevates Chanakya Bellam to whole-time director, strengthening leadership for next growth phase
author: ET Online
short_desc: This strategic move aims to bolster the company's leadership, drive accelerated growth, and enhance long-term value creation as AION-Tech scales its global ambitions.
long_desc: AION-Tech Solutions Ltd. (formerly Goldstone Technologies Limited), a publicly listed business intelligence and IT services company, has announced the elevation of Chanakya Bellam Radha Krishna to the role of Whole-Time Director on its Board. He was inducted into the Board in December 2025. This strategic appointment brings deep expertise in capital markets, enterprise strategy, fundraising, and global expansion, further strengthening the company’s leadership as it enters its next phase of growth. His appointment showcases AION-Tech’s commitment to enhanced governance, accelerated growth, and long-term value creation, as the company scales its global ambitions. In his role as Whole-Time Director, he will work closely with the leadership team to drive strategic initiatives, enhance…
